title: Fixing Inconsistent Type Inference for a Generic Math Calculator
subCategory: Generic Math Operations with Union Types

statement.markdown: Implement a generic math calculator that can perform basic arithmetic operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division) on two numbers. The calculator should support both integer and floating-point numbers. However, the current implementation has inconsistent type inference leading to incorrect results. Your task is to fix the bug and ensure the calculator produces accurate results for all input combinations.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the operation (add, subtract, multiply, divide) followed by two numbers separated by spaces.
statement.outputFormat: The result of the operation as a number.
statement.notes: The calculator should handle division by zero and return an error message in such cases.

hints.text: Pay attention to the type inference in the calculator implementation.
Use TypeScript's union types to handle both integer and floating-point numbers.

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing Inconsistent Type Inference
editorial.intuition: The key to fixing the bug lies in using TypeScript's union types to handle both integer and floating-point numbers.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force solution involves implementing separate functions for each operation and type combination.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution is to use a generic approach that can handle both integer and floating-point numbers using union types.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a generic approach with union types and handle division by zero errors correctly.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves demonstrating that the optimal solution produces accurate results for all input combinations.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run involves testing the optimal solution with various input combinations.

functionSignature.javascript: function calculate(op: string, num1: number, num2: number): number;
functionSignature.python: def calculate(self, op: str, num1: float, num2: float) -> float:
